🌱 **gardener** · ❔ `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT` · severity: `low` · commit: `n/a` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|------------|---------------|-----| | Internationalization and encoding | Reports Vietnamese text rendering issue in the UI — text appears garbled, likely a character encoding or font rendering problem | Tree has no guidance on i18n, locale support, character encoding, or non-Latin script handling | ❔ Insufficient |
Tree nodes referenced - [`engineering/frontend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — Checked stack and architecture; no mention of i18n, locale, or encoding - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Checked V1 scope and anti-requirements; i18n not addressed
### Recommendation **Why:** The context tree has no guidance on internationalization or non-Latin character support. This is a real user-facing bug, but gardener cannot assess whether i18n is in scope for V1 or how it should be handled architecturally. **Suggested path forward:** If non-English users are part of the V1 target audience, consider adding a tree node under `engineering/frontend/` or `product/` documenting i18n scope (even if minimal — e.g., "UTF-8 everywhere, no translation framework in V1"). This bug likely has a straightforward encoding fix regardless of broader i18n decisions. --- Reviewed commit: n/a · Tree snapshot: cf8868a · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|------------|---------------|-----| | DESIGN.md as design source of truth | Proposes a root-level DESIGN.md file that AI agents reference before writing UI code, inspired by Linear/getdesign.md | Tree has no guidance on a centralized design specification file or design-system-as-code approach | 🆕 New | | Typography and font choices | Specifies Inter Variable font with OpenType features cv01/ss03, 13px base size, weight 500 workhorse | Tree documents Tailwind v4 and shadcn/ui but has no typography or font decisions | 🆕 New | | Dark mode design tokens | Proposes translucent backgrounds, semi-transparent borders, luminance-stepping elevation model | Tree mentions dark-mode-native via Tailwind but no specific dark mode design token guidance | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`engineering/frontend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — Checked stack (Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui), key decisions, architecture patterns - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Checked V1 scope; UI polish not explicitly scoped in or out
### Recommendation **Why:** The tree has no design system node, so there is no documented standard to evaluate this proposal against. Introducing a DESIGN.md as a root-level agent-readable design specification is a new pattern that would shape how all future UI contributions are evaluated. **Suggested path forward:** This issue overlaps substantially with #3757 (same author, same design themes). Consider consolidating into a single tracking issue. If the DESIGN.md approach is adopted, add a corresponding tree node under `engineering/frontend/` documenting the design system decisions (font, color tokens, motion) so future PRs can be evaluated for consistency. --- Reviewed commit: n/a · Tree snapshot: cf8868a · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `medium` · commit: `n/a` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|------------|---------------|-----| | Motion and animation system | Proposes comprehensive motion tokens, entrance animations, page transitions, spring-based animations, breathing glow effects | Tree documents Tailwind v4 and shadcn/ui as the styling foundation but has no guidance on motion design, animation tokens, or transition patterns | 🆕 New | | Typography system | Proposes custom font (Inter Variable with OpenType features), specific type scale (13px workhorse), weight/tracking rules | Tree says "Tailwind v4" for styling but is silent on typography decisions, font choices, or type scale | 🆕 New | | UI component polish scope | Proposes extensive component-level redesigns across MetricCard, StatusBadge, Sidebar, FilterBar, and more | Tree documents page structure and architecture patterns but not component-level visual specifications | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`engineering/frontend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — Checked stack decisions (Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, React 19), page structure, key decisions (no global state library) - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Checked V1 scope summary and Key Product Decisions; design system polish is not in V1 scope but not explicitly deferred either
### Recommendation **Why:** The context tree has no documented design system decisions — no motion language, typography standards, or component-level visual specs. This proposal introduces a comprehensive design system that would become foundational for all future UI work, but the tree currently has no node to evaluate it against or guide its direction. **Suggested path forward:** Before implementing, consider adding a tree node under `engineering/frontend/` (e.g., `design-system.md`) that documents the chosen motion tokens, typography scale, and component patterns. This ensures future UI PRs can be evaluated for consistency against a documented standard rather than each contributor making ad-hoc decisions. Note: this issue overlaps significantly with #3756 — consolidating scope between the two would prevent duplicate/conflicting work. --- Reviewed commit: n/a · Tree snapshot: cf8868a · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `medium` · commit: `issue` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This Issue | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|------------|---------------|-----| | Concurrency policies | Proposes a new concurrency policy that checks linked issue status, not just heartbeat run status | Routines: concurrency policies (skip, queue, cancel-previous) are declared per-routine, but tree only lists run-status-based policies (product/routines) | 🆕 New | | Governance integration | Suggests skip-if-awaiting-human-review, bridging routines with approval workflows | Issue Approvals exist as a governance gate, but no tree guidance on linking routine concurrency to approval state (product/governance) | 🆕 New | | Task system interaction | Policy would inspect linked issue status to decide whether to skip | Tasks are the communication channel; this extends that contract to routine concurrency decisions (product/task-system) | ❓ Partial |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/routines/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/routines/NODE.md) — Concurrency Policies, Trigger Kinds - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Issue Approvals, Board Powers - [`product/task-system/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/task-system/NODE.md) — Issue Status Workflow, Tasks Are the Communication Channel
### Recommendation **Why:** The tree documents concurrency policies as a key routine decision but only covers run-status-based policies (skip_if_active, queue, cancel-previous). This proposal introduces a new category: issue-status-aware concurrency. The pattern of linking routine scheduling to downstream issue state (awaiting human review) crosses the routine/governance boundary and is not covered by existing tree guidance. **Suggested path forward:** Consider adding a tree node under `product/routines/` documenting the expanded concurrency policy taxonomy, including issue-status-aware policies, before implementing. This would clarify the contract between routine concurrency and issue lifecycle state. --- Reviewed commit: issue · Tree snapshot: 88dabbce · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 🌱 **gardener** · ✅ `ALIGNED` · low

**Summary:** Fixes external links in MarkdownBody to open in a new tab (`target="_blank"` with `rel="noopener noreferrer"`) instead of navigating away from the Paperclip UI. Addresses issue #3487.

**Tree context:**
- `engineering/frontend/NODE.md` — React 19 frontend. MarkdownBody is a shared renderer used across the UI.

**Observations:**
1. Minimal fix (54 additions, 2 deletions across 2 files) with test coverage.
2. Standard web security practice: `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on external links.
3. Internal issue links (matching `PREFIX-NUMBER` pattern) continue to use in-app navigation.

**Verdict:** Straightforward UX fix. No tree impact.

> tree: `88dabbce` · [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree)

🌱 **gardener** · ✅ `ALIGNED` · low

**Summary:** Fixes the opencode-local adapter to resolve config paths using `APPDATA` on Windows instead of assuming Unix-style paths. Also cleans up config path resolution logic.

**Tree context:**

### Summary This PR fixes two real bugs that prevent the Slack plugin from working correctly: 1. **Slack HMAC verification** — `express.json()` was the only body parser, so form-encoded Slack slash commands never had `rawBody` populated. HMAC verification always failed. The fix adds `express.urlencoded()` with a `verify` callback alongside the JSON parser. 2. **Plugin event duplication** — When a plugin registers multiple `ctx.events.on()` subscriptions for the same event pattern (e.g., two `issue.created` handlers), the event bus delivered N copies of the same event to the worker. The fix deduplicates via an `eventId`-based Map with a 10-second sliding window, collapsing N deliveries into 1. Both changes are **pragmatic and localized**: - They don't affect plugin process isolation, capability enforcement, or data persistence (which the tree marks as core). - The in-memory dedup cache is acceptable for transient collision prevention. - The documented gap (eventId-less events can still duplicate) is acknowledged and not a blocker. ✅ **Fit:** This PR aligns with the plugin architecture as described in the tree. It fixes bugs at the boundary between the backend webhook handler and the plugin event bus without violating the architectural constraints (process isolation, capability gating, plugin-worker contract). --- Reviewed commit: 85d8a3a · Tree snapshot: 6e68dbe · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). 🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `6363ab3b`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Direct fix for issue #3264 by the same reporter (`@harperaa`) who filed it — nice closing of the loop. The change matches the proposed fix exactly: instead of enumerating board routes and stripping only those, the new logic strips the company prefix whenever the first segment is not a global or board route. That's the right inversion — it treats plugin page routes as first-class citizens of the routing layer without requiring every new plugin to register itself in a central enumeration, which aligns with the tree's "thin core, rich edges" stance toward plugins.

The regression tests are well-structured: three separate test cases covering plugin page routes (the bug), board routes (to prove the fix doesn't regress existing behavior), and global routes (to prove the fix doesn't over-strip). The docstring explicitly links back to issue #3264, which makes the test self-documenting for future readers.

Two-line code change, 24-line test file. Perfect scope. Once this lands, issue #3264 can be closed.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `fdecac8e`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns that block merge, but a few worth noting for reviewers.

The core change is a clean single-source-of-truth refactor: three UI codepaths (`NewAgent`, `OnboardingWizard`, `AgentConfigForm`) each carried their own inline per-adapter default dictionaries, which had drifted. Extracting `adapterConfigDefaults` + `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()` eliminates the drift. Server-side normalization in `applyAdapterConfigDefaults` is actually MORE correct than UI-side defaults alone — API callers (CLI, scripts, plugins) now get the default too, not just users creating via the web UI. Good instinct.

### Notes for reviewers

1. **PR scope is broader than the description suggests.** The PR body says "UI-only default logic" and "no server, or adapter runtime changes" but the diff includes:
    - Server-side normalization in `server/src/routes/agents.ts` (`applyAdapterConfigDefaults` now writes a `dangerouslySkipPermissions` default for `claude_local` and `opencode_local`)
    - A behavior change to `asBoolean` in `packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts` (now accepts string `"true"`/`"false"` case-insensitively, was boolean-only)
    - A new 314-line `dangerously-skip-permissions.test.ts`

    Both extra changes are safe and well-tested, but the "low risk, UI-only" framing is misleading. Worth updating the PR description so CODEOWNERS/reviewers see the full scope.

2. **Interaction with PR #3294 (still open).** PR #3294 introduces `resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions()` in the claude-local adapter that overrides the configured value to `false` when Paperclip runs as root. If both PRs land, the data flow becomes:
    - UI writes `dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` from the shared default
    - Server normalizes to `true` if missing (this PR)
    - Adapter overrides to `false` at execute time on root (#3294)

    The config stored in the DB says one thing; the runtime does another. That's not a bug per se, but it creates a surprising diagnostic experience — operators will see `dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` in agent config but no `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag in the transcript. Worth coordinating with #3294 or adding a logger.info explaining the override when it fires.

3. **`asBoolean` widening is a cross-cutting behavior change.** The new string coercion (`"true"` → `true`, `"false"` → `false`, case-insensitive) affects every caller of `asBoolean` across the codebase. The widening is backwards-compatible (existing boolean inputs behave identically), and the coercion only fires on string inputs that previously fell through to the fallback. The test coverage is thorough. Low risk, but worth calling out because adapter-utils contract changes are the kind of thing that can surprise plugin authors or downstream adapters — the utility's signature lives in `@paperclipai/adapter-utils` which is part of the stable surface.

4. **`dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` as the default** is a load-bearing security decision. The inline comment explains it correctly (headless mode has no interactive prompts), but it's worth documenting in the context tree under `adapters/` as an explicit policy so future adapter authors know the rationale and don't re-litigate it.

### Recommendation

Land it. The refactor is good, the server-side enforcement is the right layer, and the tests are solid. Just (a) update the PR description to reflect the full scope, (b) add a coordination note to PR #3294 about the config-vs-runtime override interaction, and (c) consider adding a one-paragraph note to `adapters/NODE.md` or the tree about the "headless mode default skip" policy so the rationale is discoverable.

The `claude-local` adapter does not manage API keys or subscription state directly. Auth is fully delegated to the Claude CLI's own credential store (see `adapters/claude-local`). Whether a given Claude credential is subscription-based or API-credit-based is determined by the CLI — Paperclip passes through whatever auth the CLI holds.

| Billing/quota reporting | `adapters/claude-local` | Adapter reports usage from Claude stream-json output |
| API key ownership | `adapters/claude-local` | Out of scope for Paperclip |

**Recommendation:** This is a support question best answered by a maintainer or in the project's documentation/FAQ. If the answer reveals a documentation gap (e.g., subscription vs. API-credit auth modes are undocumented), consider opening a docs issue.

Both positions are defensible: - **Core argument:** Board reachability is a safety property, not a feature. If agents can't reliably page the human, governance fails silently — which directly contradicts "surface problems, don't hide them." - **Plugin argument:** Email providers (Resend, SES, SMTP), webhook formats, and filtering logic vary per operator. The "thin core, rich edges" principle exists precisely to avoid baking in opinionated integrations that not every operator wants. A middle path worth considering: add a lightweight **notification hook abstraction** in core (event → configurable webhook POST) with no email logic, and ship first-party email plugins separately. ## Summary

This PR is titled as a bug fix for plugin page route prefix-doubling, and that fix is correct and aligned. However, the PR bundles four substantial and largely unrelated changes under a misleading title: a new BastionClaw adapter (filesystem IPC + SQLite polling), a company-level kill switch (pause/resume), a heartbeat model override feature, and the actual bug fix. The BastionClaw adapter introduces a new architectural pattern not recorded in the context tree, and there is a SQL injection risk in the adapter's polling loop.

---

## Context Match

| Area | Tree | PR | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plugin page route fix (`company-routes.ts`) | Plugin extensibility is core; routing correctness required | Correctly strips company prefix for any non-global segment | ✅ Aligned |
| Company pause/resume (kill switch) | Governance: "Board can pause/resume any Agent immediately"; company lifecycle has `paused` status | Board-only `POST /:companyId/pause` + `POST /:companyId/resume`, cancels active runs | ✅ Aligned |
| Heartbeat skips paused companies | Company lifecycle: "Paused companies stop all heartbeats" | `tickTimers` now queries paused companies and skips their agents | ✅ Aligned |
| BastionClaw adapter pattern | Tree lists gateway adapters as WebSocket-based (`openclaw-gateway`); "gateway adapters connect to a remote agent over WebSocket" | BastionClaw uses **filesystem IPC + SQLite polling** — not WebSocket | ⚠️ New Territory |
| Heartbeat model override | No tree node records adapter-level model override at heartbeat invocation | `heartbeat.model` added to heartbeat policy, injected into `runtimeConfig` on timer runs only | ⚠️ New Territory |

---

## Issues

### 1. SQL injection risk in BastionClaw adapter (medium severity)

In `packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/server/execute.ts`, the task poll query interpolates `taskId` directly into an SQL string with only single-quote escaping:

```ts
`SELECT status, result, error, duration_ms FROM task_run_logs WHERE task_id = '${taskId.replace(/'/g, "''")}' ORDER BY run_at DESC LIMIT 1`
```

`taskId` is constructed as `paperclip-${ctx.runId}` where `ctx.runId` comes from the server. While the server controls `runId` today, this pattern bypasses parameterized queries and creates a brittle trust assumption. The `sqlite3` CLI used here supports parameterized queries via `-cmd`. This should use a parameterized form or at minimum assert that `taskId` contains only safe characters before interpolation.

### 2. PR bundles four unrelated changes under a misleading title (low-medium)

The title "Fix plugin page routes doubling company prefix on company switch" covers only ~10 lines of the 3,800-line diff. The rest introduces: a new adapter, a company kill switch feature, a heartbeat model override, GitHub issue templates, and a SECURITY.md. Bundling makes review harder and commit history less useful. These should be separate PRs.

### 3. BastionClaw adapter pattern is not in the tree (new territory)

The adapters tree node defines two patterns: **local** (spawns a child process) and **gateway** (WebSocket). BastionClaw uses a third pattern: **filesystem IPC + SQLite polling**. The adapter writes a JSON task file to a shared directory and polls a SQLite DB for completion. This is a novel integration pattern with meaningful tradeoffs (local-only, polling latency, SQLite lock sensitivity) that should be captured in the tree before the adapter ships, so future adapters don't re-litigate this design.

### 4. `models: []` in BastionClaw index.ts

`packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/index.ts` exports `models: []`. The tree notes that adapters report model info via `detectModel`. If BastionClaw can detect the model from the Claude instance it dispatches to, this should be wired up rather than left empty.

---

## Tree Nodes Referenced

- `adapters/NODE.md` — local vs gateway pattern, `ServerAdapterModule` interface
- `adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md` — gateway adapter precedent
- `product/governance/NODE.md` — board powers, pause/resume, budget hard-stops
- `product/company-model/NODE.md` — company lifecycle (`active`/`paused`/`archived`)
- `product/NODE.md` — control plane principles

---

## Recommendation

The routing fix and the governance/heartbeat changes are aligned and look correct. The **BastionClaw adapter** is new territory worth flagging:

1. **Fix the SQL injection** before merge — use parameterized queries or character-validation on `taskId`.
2. **Confirm the IPC+SQLite pattern is intentional** and draft a `adapters/bastionclaw/NODE.md` tree node capturing the design rationale and constraints (local-only, SQLite polling, requires BastionClaw to be co-located).
3. **Consider splitting the PR** — the bug fix and the BastionClaw adapter are independent and the bundling makes each harder to review.
